drugs.efficacy_snippet: 14.1 Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia (BPH) The efficacy of doxazosin tablets was evaluated extensively in over 900 patients with BPH in double-blind, placebo-controlled trials. Doxazosin tablets treatment was superior to placebo in improving patient symptoms and urinary flow rate. Significant relief wi

key_results: In long-term studies, improvement was maintained for up to 2 years of treatment.
Statistically significant improvements of 2.3-3.3 mL/sec in maximum flow rate were seen with doxazosin tablets in Studies 1 and 2, compared to 0.1-0.7 mL/sec with placebo.
The proportion of patients who responded with a maximum flow rate improvement of ≥ 3 mL/sec was significantly larger with doxazosin tablets (34-42%) than placebo (13-17%).
A significantly greater improvement was also seen in average flow rate with doxazosin tablets (1.6 mL/sec) than with placebo (0.2 mL/sec).

key_results: Most of the 4,325 subjects randomly assigned to receive either dutasteride or placebo completed 2 years of double-blind treatment (70% and 67%, respectively).

key_results: Most of the 4,325 subjects randomly assigned to receive either dutasteride or placebo completed 2 years of double-blind treatment (70% and 67%, respectively).
Most of the 2,340 subjects in the trial extensions completed 2 additional years of open-label treatment (71%).
Subjects receiving dutasteride achieved statistically significant improvement in symptoms versus placebo by Month 3 in 1 trial and by Month 12 in the other 2 pivotal trials.

key_results: 14.2 Overactive Bladder in Adult Males with BPH The safety, tolerability, and efficacy of GEMTESA was evaluated in a multinational 24-week, double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled trial (Study 3005, NCT03902080) in male patients at least 45 years of age with OAB on pharmacological therapy (i.e., treatment with an alpha blocker, with or without a 5-alpha reductase inhibitor) for BPH.
A total of 1105 patients were randomized 1:1 to receive either GEMTESA 75 mg or placebo once daily for 24 weeks.

key_results: Results from these studies indicated that terazosin statistically significantly improved symptoms and peak urine flow rates over placebo (Table 6).

Analysis of the effect of terazosin on individual urinary symptoms demonstrated that terazosin significantly improved the symptoms of hesitancy, intermittency, impairment in size and force of urinary stream, sensation of incomplete emptying, terminal dribbling, daytime frequency and nocturia, compared to placebo.
